prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial has a warm red/orange, vintage-looking tint throughout, with a white “Nostalgic X’er” watermark at the lower right in most frames.

At the beginning, several children are shown outdoors in a dim red-tinted scene. They appear to be moving or playing together. The next shots show a blonde girl and a blonde boy in a striped top closer to the camera, then a wider view of children on a paved area near steps and brickwork. Two children appear to be balancing or moving on skates, with arms extended as if trying to keep steady.

The focus then shifts to an injury or scraped knee. There is an extreme close-up of a rounded knee area with a bandage or dressing-like material visible. A close-up follows of a blond boy wearing dark-framed glasses, looking downward or toward the injury. Another close shot shows children leaning in closely over a knee; the boy with glasses is partly visible, and another child’s face is near the top of the frame. The knee is centered between them.

A close-up of another blond boy’s face follows; his cheek/nose area appears freckled or possibly scraped, though the image is soft and tinted. Then a blonde girl looks closely at a bent knee. On the knee or dressing, the word “OUCHLESS!” is visible in dark letters, suggesting attention to a painless or gentle pad/dressing.

Later, the commercial cuts to a product/package close-up. Large printed text reads: “‘OUCHLESS’” with smaller text below reading “TELFA pad.” The shot is tightly framed on the packaging label. Another close-up shows a white pad or dressing being placed or shown against skin, though the exact action is partially unclear due to the tight framing and soft image.

The final frames show a product brand graphic: the word “KENDALL” in bold black lettering above a simple block design made of rectangular shapes. This image remains static through the last frame.
